items.objective: Follow and complete a short route-finding dialogue between a visitor and a local.
items.instructions: Read the exchange below. Some of the visitor's lines are missing. Choose the correct line from the options to complete each gap. The visitor is asking for the bazaar and the bus stop.
successCriteria: Open with a polite attention-getter before asking a question.
Ask correctly where the bazaar is using кайда.
Confirm the bus route using ушул автобус барабы.
items.completionHint: Remember to start with Кечиресиз before asking a stranger a question.

supportCards.title: Destination suffix recap
supportCards.body: Базар → базарга (to the bazaar). Үй → үйгө (to home). Add -га after a voiced back consonant, -ка after a voiceless back consonant, -ге after a voiced front consonant, -ке after a voiceless front consonant.

supportCards.title: Direction words flow quickly
supportCards.body: Солго and оңго (to the left / to the right) are each said as a single natural unit. Do not pause between the direction word and its -го ending.

supportCards.title: Asking for the bazaar is a power move
supportCards.body: In almost any Kyrgyz town or city, asking Базар кайда? is the single most useful navigation question. Markets are central, well-known, and always someone's destination. From the bazaar, you can orient yourself to find almost anything else.

turns.speaker: Visitor
turns.translation: Excuse me, where is the bazaar?

turns.speaker: Local
turns.text: Базар жакын. Түз жүрүңүз, андан кийин оңго буруңуз.
turns.transliteration: Bazaar zhakyn. Tüz zhürüngüz, andan kiyin onggo burunguz.
turns.translation: The bazaar is close. Go straight, then turn right.

turns.speaker: Visitor
turns.translation: Thank you! Where is the bus stop?

turns.speaker: Local
turns.translation: The stop is not far, to the left.

turns.speaker: Visitor
turns.translation: Does this bus go to the bazaar?
